title: Ecbolium insulare

This family is commonly known as the acanthus or thorny flower family. The genus Ecbolium is a small group of plants native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. intro: Ecbolium insulare, also known as the Island Ecbolium or Island Lamiacene, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ecbolium insulare include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ecbolium insulare is often used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
